title: 05157_Specializing_Smaller_Language_Models_towards_Multi-Step_Reasoning
positive_examples: Specializing smaller models allows a larger range of researchers and practitioners to access multi-step reasoning capabilities, which were previously limited to larger models.
Improved performance on math reasoning tasks can enhance educational tools and resources, making them more effective for learners.
negative_examples: Decreased performance on generic tasks (BigBench Hard) as a result of specialization, which may limit the model's versatility in other applications.
The specialization process may lead to overfitting on specific tasks, reducing the model's ability to generalize to new or varied tasks.
